name: Snowflake Pancakes with Blueberries
recipeIngredient: 1 Cake yeast or 1/4 oz Package dry yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the batter the night before serving. Soften yeast in warm water (if using a cake, water should be around 80°F; if using dry yeast, heat water to between 105°F and 115°F). In a separate container, mix together buttermilk, baking soda, sugar, and salt. Add the softened yeast to this mixture. Sift flour and baking powder together and add with oil to the buttermilk mixture. Mix well. Fold in eggs.
2. Place the batter in a large container and refrigerate overnight to allow it to double in size.
3. In the morning, stir blueberries into the batter.
4. Cook pancakes in a frying pan over medium heat and serve.
5. This recipe will keep in the refrigerator for up to a week. If black specks appear in the batter, they are yeast mold and do not harm the mixture.
